target: data/wins.json WIN-025 detail_evidence paragraph 4 — add FSF setup so the cluster-vocabulary verdict (EXP-229) has an evidence-level premise. Closes curmudgeon WIN-025 c3 Hole 3 (detail_evidence and detail_verdict_text disjointed post-EXP-229).
patch_instructions: Single substring replacement in the WIN with id='025' in data/wins.json, field detail_evidence. Replaces the fourth paragraph (the 'no derived prediction' paragraph) with a revised paragraph that introduces FSF = H(r)/r, the δZ = baseline × coverage × FSF scaling equation, and concedes the station-to-station ratio as a genuine geometric derivation — giving the cluster-aligned detail_verdict_text (post-EXP-229) an evidence-level premise to land on. Preserves the monitor.py 2026 forward prediction detail and the 'relabels as aetheric' closing beat. No other WIN-025 fields are touched.

patches.before: The dome provides no derived prediction. The dome's monitor.py hardcodes a prospective 2026 eclipse prediction (ΔB = −18.22 × 0.95 × 1.672 ≈ −29 nT) but shows no derivation from dome geometry, aetheric medium properties, or firmament conductivity. The dome model observes that eclipses produce ~10 nT variations — known since the 1930s — and relabels the cause as 'aetheric.'
patches.after: The dome's scaling has geometric content, but not at the magnitude level. The dome publishes a scaling equation δZstation = baseline × eclipse-coverage × FSF, where FSF = H(r)/r is a station-to-station factor derived from dome geometry (firmament height H divided by the station's dome-coordinate radius r). At the cluster level this ratio reproduces the observed 9-station pattern to Pearson r ≈ 0.9999 — we grant it as a genuine geometric derivation (verified independently in the WIN-010/WIN-068 cluster). What the dome does not derive is the absolute magnitude: the 'baseline' term is imported from the 2017 Boulder single-station observation, not from any dome parameter. The dome's monitor.py hardcodes a prospective 2026 eclipse prediction (ΔB = −18.22 × 0.95 × 1.672 ≈ −29 nT) on top of this imported baseline but derives no magnitude from firmament height, aetheric medium properties, or firmament conductivity. The dome model observes that eclipses produce ~10 nT variations — known since the 1930s — relabels the cause as 'aetheric,' and scales the station-to-station pattern with a geometric ratio while importing the absolute magnitude from observation.
patches.rationale: Curmudgeon WIN-025 c3 Hole 3 (moderate): detail_evidence (unchanged by EXP-229) still told the pre-concession story — 'shows no derivation from dome geometry' — while detail_verdict_text (rewritten by EXP-229) now concedes FSF = H(r)/r is a genuine geometric derivation. A reader expanding Evidence then Verdict saw two arguments about essentially different things, and the verdict's FSF-retraction clause had no premise in Evidence. The new P4 introduces the scaling equation and the station-to-station ratio one paragraph before the verdict concedes it, so the retraction lands on an evidence-level claim. The substantive critiques (no magnitude derivation from dome parameters, baseline imported from the 2017 Boulder observation, monitor.py hardcode, 'relabels as aetheric') all survive intact.

holes_addressed.hole: WIN-025 c3 Hole 3 (moderate) — detail_evidence and detail_verdict_text disjointed post-EXP-229
holes_addressed.description: Pre-EXP-229, detail_verdict_text said the dome 'provides no independent derivation or mechanism' — consistent with detail_evidence P4's 'no derivation from dome geometry.' Post-EXP-229, detail_verdict_text concedes FSF = H(r)/r is a genuine geometric derivation (Pearson r ≈ 0.9999) and retracts 'our earlier characterization of FSF as empirical.' But detail_evidence never introduced FSF, the scaling equation δZ = baseline × coverage × FSF, or the station-to-station ratio, so the verdict's retraction pointed at a claim the evidence had not made. The curmudgeon flagged this as 'we apologize for a claim we never made,' and preferred option (a): add a short FSF setup paragraph to evidence rather than stripping the cluster concession from the verdict.
holes_addressed.fix: Replace the existing detail_evidence P4 with a revised paragraph that (i) names the scaling equation δZ = baseline × coverage × FSF, (ii) names FSF = H(r)/r and identifies it as a dome-geometric ratio, (iii) concedes the Pearson r ≈ 0.9999 station-to-station fit as a genuine geometric derivation with a cluster cross-reference to WIN-010/WIN-068, (iv) distinguishes derived-ratio from imported-baseline, and (v) preserves the monitor.py 2026 prediction detail and the 'relabels as aetheric' closing beat. The verdict's retraction now lands on a premise the evidence supplies directly.

new_evidence_added: FSF = H(r)/r derivation already verified in the WIN-010/WIN-068 cluster (EXP-220, EXP-222) — no independent recomputation claimed here. The concession is cross-referenced from WIN-025 for cluster consistency.
δZ = baseline × coverage × FSF scaling equation — already carried in detail_verdict_text post-EXP-229; imported into detail_evidence verbatim so the verdict lands on an evidence-level premise.

anticipated_objections.objection: Adding the FSF concession to detail_evidence re-introduces the cluster-import problem curmudgeon c3 Hole 2 flagged (target-drift: WIN-025 critique centered on 2026 architecture, but WIN-025's claim is about 2024 observation). Doesn't this make the Hole 2 problem worse?
anticipated_objections.response: The new P4 makes the WIN-025-specific point that FSF scales the station-to-station pattern — which is exactly the 9-station 2024 data WIN-025 is about — and concedes that part. It distinguishes the station-scaling (dome-geometric, specific to the 2024 9-station pattern) from the absolute magnitude (imported from 2017). So the FSF concession is now anchored to the WIN-025 claim, not the 2026 forward prediction. Hole 2's target-drift concern applies primarily to the 'finding' field and tldr_verdict; this evidence-level edit pulls the WIN-025-specific framing toward the 9-station pattern. A follow-up EXP can tighten the finding field along the same lines.

anticipated_objections.objection: The revised P4 is substantially longer than the original (one paragraph → one paragraph, but roughly doubled in words). Is that dilution?
anticipated_objections.response: The length increase carries the FSF setup — without it, the verdict's retraction clause is orphaned. The alternative (option b per curmudgeon: strip FSF-retraction from detail_verdict_text) would leave WIN-025 silent on FSF while WIN-010 and WIN-068 concede it, reopening the defender quote-mine EXP-229 was designed to close. Option (a) is the cluster-consistent choice; the word budget is load-bearing.

anticipated_objections.objection: Why not also add the dome's own evolution-log admission (pre-v45: 'Incorrectly predicted 2024 eclipse magnitudes identically across all latitudes') while the file is open? Curmudgeon c3 Hole 1 rates it 'major.'
anticipated_objections.response: Hole 1 is explicitly flagged by the curmudgeon as a separate EXP, because the evolution-log admission applies to the WIN-010/WIN-025/WIN-068 cluster uniformly and should be propagated in one cluster-wide pass. Mixing a cluster-wide argument into an EXP-235 scoped at H3 would under-propagate (WIN-010 and WIN-068 would still be missing it) and cost a later consolidation edit. Tracker note for EXP-235 restricts scope to H3; Hole 1 is queued as the next cluster pass.

anticipated_objections.objection: Doesn't adding 'we grant it as a genuine geometric derivation' in detail_evidence double-state the concession (it's already in detail_verdict_text post-EXP-229)?
anticipated_objections.response: It is deliberately double-stated. Evidence states the claim and the concession; Verdict retracts the earlier characterization. Readers who skim only the evidence section need the concession there too, or they carry away the pre-EXP-229 stance. The concession appearing in both places is how the verdict's retraction has something to point to, which was the whole purpose of H3.

kernel_of_truth: The underlying geometric insight is real: if the dome has a well-defined firmament height H and each station has a well-defined dome-coordinate radius r, then H(r)/r is a genuine function of dome geometry and will produce a specific station-to-station ratio. The Pearson r ≈ 0.9999 cluster-level verification (WIN-010/WIN-068) is not a claim we can dismiss. The concession belongs in detail_evidence, not just detail_verdict_text, because the evidence section is where we establish what the dome actually publishes. What the concession does not do is rescue the absolute magnitude — that is still imported from observation, and the mechanism that produces the magnitude (Chapman 1933 Sq current disruption) is standard physics regardless of the dome-coordinate scaling law layered on top.
out_of_scope_deliberately: Curmudgeon WIN-025 c3 Hole 1 (major) — dome's own evolution-log post-hoc admission. Belongs in a cluster-wide EXP covering WIN-010/WIN-025/WIN-068, not this WIN-025-local edit.
Curmudgeon WIN-025 c3 Hole 2 (moderate) — finding field 2026-baseline target-drift. Separate EXP targeting the finding field.
Curmudgeon WIN-025 c3 Hole 4 (moderate) — closing triple-count sentence tightening (mechanism vs data).
Curmudgeon WIN-025 c3 Hole 5 (minor) — tldr_verdict self-containedness trade-off. No change recommended; EXP-229 accepted the trade-off deliberately.
Curmudgeon WIN-025 c3 Hole 6 (minor) — Kim & Chang 2018 publisher-page citation audit (cluster-wide task).
Curmudgeon WIN-025 c3 Hole 7 (minor) — Part 2b derives_from_dome aggregation-note (not a WIN-025 task).
cross_win_consistency_flag: After integration, WIN-025 detail_evidence and detail_verdict_text will tell the same story: the dome publishes a scaling equation with a station-to-station ratio derived from dome geometry (conceded), while the absolute magnitude is imported from the 2017 observation (critiqued). WIN-010 and WIN-068 already sit at this consistency level; this edit brings WIN-025 into line at the evidence level too. WIN-068 finding-field parallel fix remains outstanding per EXP-229 propagation_note.
propagation_note: This EXP closes curmudgeon c3 Hole 3 specifically. The cluster-wide evolution-log admission (c3 Hole 1) and the WIN-025 finding-field target-drift (c3 Hole 2) are next in the queue.
